D6 Display L4 Analyze Reference

Gamut Coverage Percentages

"95% DCI-P3" means the display's triangle overlaps that fraction of the standard's area — coverage, not the display's own size

A spec like "99% sRGB, 90% P3" reports coverage: how much of each reference gamut the display's triangle actually contains. It is the overlap area divided by the reference area, capped at 100%. A display can exceed sRGB in area yet still miss a corner — so coverage, not raw size, is what gets quoted.

Grow the display gamut from sRGB toward Rec. 2020 and watch each reference's coverage climb. Highlight a reference to see exactly which triangle is being measured against on the chromaticity chart.
